name: Hunter Williams
title: Founder, FRC Peptides; Co-author, 2023 Peptide Cheat Sheet
bio: Hunter Williams is the founder of FRC Peptides and co-author with Jay Campbell of the 2023 Peptide Cheat Sheet. A former athlete who hit a wall in his mid-20s with depression, anxiety, and the loss of feeling himself despite a lifetime of fitness, Hunter discovered hormone optimization and peptide therapy and rebuilt from the inside. He hosts The Hunter Williams Podcast — clinical, practitioner-voiced takes on peptides, hormones, GLP-1 protocols, oral bioavailability, and the daily decisions of optimization. His voice in the library is direct, comparative, and fast to call out what is overrated.
credentials: Co-author with Jay Campbell, 2023 Peptide Cheat Sheet

signature_topics.topic: GLP-1 protocols and stacking (semaglutide / tirzepatide / retatrutide / cagrilintide)
signature_topics.evidence: 442 mentions of GLP-1 + 508 retatrutide + dedicated mailbag and microdose-vs-real-dose episodes
1,441 mentions across podcast + cheat sheet — his most-covered topic
264 BPC-157 + 304 TB-500 mentions; KPV positioned as his #1 anti-inflammatory

signature_topics.topic: Oral peptide bioavailability and what he considers obsolete
signature_topics.evidence: Cheat sheet co-author voice; calls Sermorelin obsolete, AOD9604 overrated, HGH frag worse than AOD
310 SS-31 + 300 NAD mentions; warns MOTS-c can backfire if overused

voice_quote: Hey everybody, this is Hunter Williams. I hope you're doing amazing wherever you are at in the world... I was an athlete growing up. So I was obsessed with becoming the best football player that I could and making it to the NFL. That didn't work out. So I had to get a normal job like everyone else, but somewhere around the age of 25, I really started to notice that I just wasn't myself. I'd been fit my whole life and I was depressed, I was anxious, and I didn't feel good.
voice_quote_citation: Hunter Williams (p0IMKbfAyFo 00:00:00–00:00:42): "Welcome! Here's What You Need to Know About Me, Hunter Williams" (2026-04-03)
